spec.title: Style guide persistence and version events

humanSummary.text: Initial audit found P1 blockers in style-guide event durability and stale approval enforcement. Repair commits d2e07a2 and 552b3ad fixed both blockers. Focused re-audit found no remaining P0/P1 findings; two P2 hardening findings were converted into follow-up DAG nodes.

findings.title: Style-guide version pointers lack same-branch DB constraints
findings.description: latest_version_id, approved_version_id, and previous_version_id remain plain text, while version rows independently carry style guide, project, and locale branch identifiers.

evidence.detail: Focused re-audit verified repository/service guards but found no DB-level constraints preventing dangling or cross-branch/project pointers outside repository writes.

findings.impact: Direct SQL or future repository bugs could create style-guide version pointers that cross locale branches or projects.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Style-guide guide/version/project/locale branch consistency is enforced by deferrable foreign keys, composite constraints, triggers, or equivalent migration-level checks.
Migration contract tests reject dangling latest/approved/previous version references and cross-branch/project version rows.

proposedDagNode.title: Style guide version DB consistency constraints
proposedDagNode.summary: Add migration-level consistency constraints or equivalent checks for style-guide latest, approved, previous, project, and locale-branch version relationships.
acceptanceCriteria: Dangling latest, approved, and previous version references are rejected.
Cross-project or cross-locale-branch style-guide version references are rejected.
Valid repository-created style-guide versions and approvals continue to migrate and test successfully.
auditFocus: Cross-branch style policy leakage through direct DB writes

findings.title: Style-guide event payload shape is not DB-enforced
findings.description: The outbox payload column accepts generic JSONB; migration 0015 adds the style-guide event type but does not enforce the event payload schema at the DB boundary.

evidence.detail: Focused re-audit found TypeScript payload typing but no event-type-specific JSONB constraint or typed event table.

findings.impact: Future producers could enqueue malformed style guide events without source revision or version ids, weakening event-driven consumers.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: StyleGuideVersionChanged outbox payloads are validated by a DB JSONB check, typed event table, or durable validator contract.
Invalid payloads missing schemaVersion, project id, locale branch id, previous/new version ids, or source revision reference are rejected in tests.

proposedDagNode.title: Style guide event payload DB contract
proposedDagNode.summary: Enforce or durably validate the StyleGuideVersionChanged outbox payload schema at the event persistence boundary.
deliverables: Style-guide event payload DB or validator contract
acceptanceCriteria: StyleGuideVersionChanged payloads require schemaVersion, project id, locale branch id, previous/new version ids, and source revision reference.
Malformed style-guide event payloads fail before consumers can process them.
Existing valid style-guide event fixtures continue to pass.
